# Cross-Species OGT Analysis

**N proteins:** 34,231 across 13 organisms (OGT 15–70 °C)

## 1. Organism-level trend
Spearman ρ(mean Z, OGT) = **+0.212** (parametric p = 0.486; permutation p = **0.4951**, 10,000 shuffles; n = 13 organisms).

Phylogenetic note: the 13 qualifying organisms span 10 coarse phylogenetic groups (Archaea, Deinococcus-Thermus, Firmicutes, Fungi, Human body fluids, Insecta, Nematoda, Proteobacteria, Vertebrata, Viridiplantae), giving an effective independent sample size of approximately 10. PGLS correction was not applied; the permutation p above should be interpreted with this caveat.

![org](data:image/png;base64,)

## 2. LOO robustness
Dropping each organism in turn: ρ range [+0.000, +0.329] (full ρ = +0.212).
Signal flips when at least one organism is dropped — see table.

| Dropped organism | ρ(mean Z, OGT) |
|-----------------|---------------|
| Oleispira antarctica | +0.286 |
| Caenorhabditis elegans | +0.279 |
| Arabidopsis thaliana | +0.279 |
| Danio rerio | +0.257 |
| Drosophila melanogaster | +0.193 |
| Bacillus subtilis | +0.175 |
| Saccharomyces cerevisiae | +0.260 |
| Escherichia coli | +0.081 |
| Homo sapiens | +0.329 |
| Human body fluids | +0.297 |
| Mus musculus | +0.297 |
| Picrophilus torridus | +0.000 |
| Thermus thermophilus | +0.000 |

![loo](data:image/png;base64,)

## 3. Z-metric consistency
All three Z metrics show the same direction of association with OGT.

| Metric | ρ(mean metric, OGT) | parametric p |
|--------|--------------------:|-------------:|
| z_mean | +0.212 | 0.486 |
| z_max | +0.162 | 0.597 |
| z_frac | +0.154 | 0.616 |

## 4. MIN_PER_ORG sensitivity

| MIN_PER_ORG | Organisms qualifying | ρ(mean Z, OGT) |
|------------:|--------------------:|---------------:|
| 30 | 13 | +0.212 |
| 50 | 13 | +0.212 |
| 100 | 13 | +0.212 |
| 200 | 12 | +0.297 |

## 5. Within-species replication

pooled (organism-demeaned) protein-level Spearman ρ(Z, Tm) = **+0.079** (p = 1.26e-48, N = 34,231 proteins).

| Organism | OGT | N | ρ(z_mean, Tm) | p |
|----------|----:|--:|--------------:|---|
| Oleispira antarctica | 15 | 1261 | +0.038 | 1.73e-01 |
| Caenorhabditis elegans | 20 | 3032 | +0.041 | 2.31e-02 |
| Arabidopsis thaliana | 25 | 2380 | +0.098 | 1.69e-06 |
| Danio rerio | 28 | 1786 | +0.078 | 1.04e-03 |
| Drosophila melanogaster | 28 | 1560 | -0.026 | 3.10e-01 |
| Bacillus subtilis | 30 | 1354 | +0.149 | 3.88e-08 |
| Saccharomyces cerevisiae | 30 | 1757 | +0.086 | 2.87e-04 |
| Escherichia coli | 37 | 1987 | +0.109 | 1.14e-06 |
| Homo sapiens | 37 | 10695 | +0.086 | 5.45e-19 |
| Human body fluids | 37 | 100 | -0.095 | 3.45e-01 |
| Mus musculus | 37 | 6434 | +0.081 | 6.91e-11 |
| Picrophilus torridus | 60 | 767 | +0.031 | 3.85e-01 |
| Thermus thermophilus | 70 | 1118 | +0.087 | 3.46e-03 |

![within](data:image/png;base64,)

## 6. Composition control (headline)
Note: partial Spearman and nested regression are computed at protein level (N ≈ 34k), not organism level; OGT enters as a per-protein label inherited from organism membership.
- Raw Spearman ρ(z_mean, OGT) = -0.041
- **Partial** ρ(z_mean, OGT | charged_frac, net_charge_per_res, surface_charge_density) = **+0.006** (p = 2.98e-01)
- Nested model of Tm: R²(composition) = 0.0086 → R²(+z_mean) = 0.0124; **ΔR² = +0.0038**, added-term p = 3.67e-30, semi-standardized z_mean coef (X-standardized) = +0.688.

![delta](data:image/png;base64,)

**Verdict:** Z does NOT retain a significant positive association with thermal stability after controlling for bulk charge composition.
